_note: Engagement-posture manifest for every L2 intent template. FORCES classification: any new or re-versioned src/.../templates/L2_intents/*.json must have a (re-confirmed) entry here or tests/test_l2_engagement_manifest.py turns the build red. This is the systemic control the 2026-06-14 engagement rewrite (PR #4) lacked, which is why general_chat got the engagement treatment while info_request/exit_skill/new_skill/low_confidence were silently left on the pre-engagement template. Lives in prompts/ NOT templates/ because the template loader recursively globs templates/**/*.json (loader.py) and would try to load a manifest there as a template. When a template's version changes, re-review its posture and bump classified_against_version.

_posture_definitions.engagement_bridge: Warm, validate-before-inform, closes with a light conditional bridge/invitation (the engagement-layer contract).
_posture_definitions.terse_by_design: Deliberately short and firm; warmth/engagement would be wrong here (safety/refusal/persona surfaces).
_posture_definitions.clarifying_contract: A short clarifying turn whose single open question IS the bridge; do not add a second bridge; length-capped.
_posture_definitions.l3_owned: Engagement is delivered by the L3 skill step, not the L2 frame; the L2 template only holds the thread.

crisis.rationale: Crisis response is short, direct, resource-first (IE crisis target ~20-40 words); engagement framing is inappropriate.

jailbreak.rationale: Persona reassertion / refusal; must not engage or bridge.

scope_refusal.rationale: Out-of-scope decline; brief and firm by design.

low_confidence.rationale: Ambiguous turn; one gentle clarifying question that is itself the bridge, capped at two sentences (safety-coupled to the suppressed freeflow guardrail). Composer-migrated in PR #124; content ratified 2026-07-07 (v1.1.0, byte-identical to #124 frozen text).

skill_continuation.rationale: Mid-skill continuation; the L2 frame only holds the current thread, while validation/engagement for the turn is delivered by the active L3 skill step. A bridge in L2 here would compete with the L3 protocol.

general_chat.rationale: Open conversation; carries the full engagement-then-bridge contract (v1.5.0, the PR #4 rewrite).

general_chat_directive.rationale: general_chat variant with directive posture (lead with concrete suggestions); engagement-oriented, no closing question by design, still warm.

general_chat_advicefirst.rationale: Advice-first general_chat variant; engagement-oriented. Draft variant (v0.1.0-draft) — reclassify on promotion.

info_request.rationale: Single-intent info_request closes with one open clarifying QUESTION (Abby-style triage) enabled by the D4 amendment; posture re-confirmed at v2.1.0 (clinical ruling 2026-07-07, turn-aware both/and).

info_request_repeat.rationale: Repeat/consecutive info_request (lookup mode) variant: statement bridge (retained Option i) so a user in a run of factual questions is not re-triaged every turn. Selected via prev_primary_intent.

new_skill.rationale: Introduces a support approach; conditional-invitation close where a next step fits. v1.1.0 promoted + posture re-confirmed 2026-07-07.

new_skill_unmatched.rationale: User asked for help with no skill match; engagement-oriented unmatched-disclosure framing. Draft variant (v0.1.0-draft) — reclassify on promotion.

exit_skill.rationale: Warm transition out of a skill into open conversation with a conditional invitation. v1.1.0 promoted + posture re-confirmed 2026-07-07.

skill_offer.rationale: Consent-gated offer of a skill; warm, non-coercive engagement framing (approved 2026-06-16).

skill_offer_reoffer.rationale: Lighter re-ask of a live offer; engagement-oriented, avoids repeated-script feel (approved 2026-06-16).
